title: Extended Datacube Dimension
description: Extends the STAC Datacube Extension dimension object with size and provider properties for scalable dimension member dissemination. Full provider details (config, parameters, hierarchy, language support, capabilities) are exposed at the OGC API - Dimensions collection endpoint referenced by provider.href; the paginated items endpoint is discovered via the OGC API - Records rel="items" link on that collection response.

type.description: Dimension type. The values 'spatial', 'temporal', and 'bands' are defined by the STAC Datacube Extension. This specification additionally proposes 'nominal' (unordered coded dimension whose members are named categories, such as administrative units or statistical indicator codes) and 'ordinal' (ordered coded dimension whose members have an inherent rank, such as quality flags or severity levels). Existing implementations that do not recognise 'nominal' or 'ordinal' treat them as unknown and continue to process the dimension using other standard properties. The value 'other' remains valid for dimensions that do not fit any of the named types.

oneOf.description: Proposed additions: coded dimensions (nominal = unordered categories; ordinal = ranked categories).

extent.description: The extent (minimum and maximum values) of the dimension. For hierarchical dimensions, this represents the bounds of the entire dimension across all levels.

values.description: Explicit enumeration of dimension members. OPTIONAL when provider is present.

step.description: The space between dimension values. Use null for irregular spacing. Supports ISO 8601 durations for standard intervals. For non-Gregorian intervals (dekadal, pentadal), set step to null and rely on the provider to define member boundaries.

unit.description: Unit of measure. Reference UCUM or OGC Definition URIs.

reference_system.description: The spatial or temporal reference system.

size.description: Total number of discrete members in this dimension. For flat dimensions, this is the total member count. For hierarchical dimensions with leveled strategy, omit this field and use size on individual levels instead; for recursive strategy, retrieve total count from the provider. RECOMMENDED for flat and recursive dimensions.

provider.description: Lightweight reference to the dimension's provider — the system that serves or generates the members. Contains just enough to categorize the dimension and link to the full definition at the OGC API - Dimensions endpoint. Full details (config, parameters, output schema, invertible flag, hierarchy, language support, conformance) are available by following provider.href.

type.description: Provider type identifier. Well-known types: 'daily-period', 'integer-range', 'static-tree', 'leveled-tree'. Custom providers use a full URI.

href.description: Link to the dimension collection at the OGC API - Dimensions endpoint. Following this link returns the full provider definition, hierarchy structure, language support, conformsTo URIs, and capability links (/inverse, /children, /ancestors).

provider.description: When provider is present, the dimension has a backing service. Follow provider.href to discover capabilities (invertible, hierarchical, language support). When the provider type is 'static-tree' or 'leveled-tree', size SHOULD be specified per hierarchy level rather than at the dimension root.
